name: Kurdistan Region of Iraq (هەرێمی کوردستان / إقليم كردستان)
summary: The Kurdistan Region of Iraq (هەرێمی کوردستان Herêmî Kurdistan in Kurdish / إقليم كردستان Iqlīm Kurdistān in Arabic) is a Northern Iraqi mixed civil-law/Sharia-law constitutionally-recognised semi-autonomous region of the Federal Republic of Iraq — structurally distinctive globally as the only sub-state-level Kurdish jurisdiction with formal constitutional autonomy framework (Constitution of Iraq 2005 Article 117 formally recognising Kurdistan Region as constitutionally-recognised federal region — distinct from rest-of-Kurdish-population frameworks in Turkey, Syria, Iran where Kurdish-population sub-state-level autonomy is contested), as the only sub-state-level Kurdish jurisdiction with continuous de facto independence framework predating constitutional recognition (Kurdistan Regional Government KRG operations continuously since 1991 No-Fly Zone framework establishment 6 April 1991 following Gulf War 1991 — 14 years of de facto autonomy 1991-2005 prior to formal constitutional recognition), as the central jurisdiction of the contested 2017 Kurdistan independence referendum framework (Kurdistan Independence Referendum 25 September 2017 — 92.73% YES with 72.16% turnout, ~2.86 million YES votes out of ~3.31 million total — characterised by Iraqi Federal Government + Iran + Turkey as unilateral non-binding action), and as the central jurisdiction of one of the most distinctive modern post-Saddam constitutional federal frameworks (post-2003 Iraq War + 2005 Iraqi Constitution federal framework + Iraqi Federal Supreme Court 2022 ruling rendering 2017 referendum unconstitutional). The Kurdistan Region historically operated under various Kurdish emirate frameworks (Soran Emirate 1816-1836 + Botan Emirate + Baban Emirate) + Ottoman vilayet framework + British Mandate Iraq 1920-1932 + Iraqi Kingdom 1932-1958 + Iraqi Republic 1958-2003 (including Anfal genocide 1986-1989 + Halabja chemical attack 16 March 1988 with ~5,000 deaths), achieved de facto autonomy 1991 following Gulf War No-Fly Zone establishment, established Kurdistan Regional Government 1992, achieved formal constitutional recognition via Iraqi Constitution 2005 Article 117, conducted 2017 Independence Referendum with 92.73% YES, and continues to operate as constitutionally-recognised federal region. Kurdistan Region of Iraq comprises ~46,861 km² (formal) / ~78,736 km² (with disputed territories) with a population of ~6.2 million. Family-law framework operates under a dual federal Iraqi civil-law framework (Iraqi Personal Status Law 188/1959) + Kurdistan-specific Family-Law Amendment 15/2008 framework. The Kurdistan Federal Court is the apex regional court; final appellate jurisdiction is contested between the Federal Supreme Court of Iraq + Kurdistan Federal Court. Kurdistan is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label. Iraq is a Hague Convention 1980 signatory but not a party — Kurdistan is governed by the Iraqi non-ratification status.

statutory_framework.citation: Constitution of Iraq 2005 Article 117 (Kurdistan Region Federal Recognition)
statutory_framework.title: Constitution of Iraq Article 117
statutory_framework.relevance: Iraqi Constitutional Article 117 formally recognising Kurdistan Region as constitutionally-recognised federal region — only sub-state-level Kurdish jurisdiction with formal constitutional autonomy framework.

statutory_framework.citation: Iraqi Personal Status Law 188/1959 + Kurdistan Family-Law Amendment 15/2008
statutory_framework.title: Iraqi Personal Status Law + Kurdistan Amendment
statutory_framework.relevance: Iraqi Federal Personal Status Law 188/1959 applicable in Kurdistan Region + Kurdistan-specific Family-Law Amendment 15/2008 modifying family-law framework for Kurdistan Region.

statutory_framework.title: Kurdistan Independence Referendum
statutory_framework.relevance: Kurdistan Independence Referendum 25 September 2017 — 92.73% YES with 72.16% turnout — characterised by Iraqi Federal Government + Iran + Turkey as unilateral non-binding action.

statutory_framework.citation: Iraqi Federal Supreme Court 2022 Ruling on 2017 Referendum
statutory_framework.title: Iraqi Federal Supreme Court 2022 Ruling
statutory_framework.relevance: Iraqi Federal Supreme Court ruling 22 February 2022 declaring 2017 Kurdistan Independence Referendum unconstitutional.

statutory_framework.citation: UN Security Council Resolution 688/1991 + No-Fly Zone framework
statutory_framework.title: UNSC Resolution 688 + No-Fly Zone
statutory_framework.relevance: UN Security Council Resolution 688 of 5 April 1991 + US-UK-France No-Fly Zone establishment 6 April 1991 — basis for de facto Kurdistan autonomy 1991-2005.

apex_courts.name: Kurdistan Federal Court
apex_courts.role: Apex regional appellate court for civil and criminal matters from Kurdistan Region — contested with Federal Supreme Court of Iraq.

apex_courts.name: Federal Supreme Court of Iraq
apex_courts.role: Federal Supreme Court of Iraq — contested jurisdiction over Kurdistan Region matters.

professional_regulators.name: Kurdistan Regional Government Ministry of Health
professional_regulators.role: Kurdistan Regional professional regulatory framework applicable in Kurdistan Region.

anonymisation_convention: Kurdistan family-court decisions are anonymised per Iraqi + Kurdistan court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Anfal genocide begins
key_developments.description: Anfal genocide 1986-1989 — Saddam Hussein's Iraqi Government anti-Kurdish military campaign with ~50,000-182,000 Kurdish deaths.

key_developments.title: Halabja chemical attack
key_developments.description: Halabja chemical attack 16 March 1988 — ~5,000 Kurdish civilian deaths via Iraqi chemical weapons.

key_developments.title: Gulf War + No-Fly Zone + de facto autonomy
key_developments.description: Gulf War 1991 ceasefire + UNSC Resolution 688 5 April 1991 + No-Fly Zone establishment 6 April 1991 — basis for de facto Kurdistan autonomy 1991-2005.

key_developments.title: Kurdistan Regional Government established
key_developments.description: Kurdistan Regional Government KRG formally established 4 July 1992 — first formal Kurdish self-government framework in modern Iraq.

key_developments.title: Iraq War + Saddam regime collapse
key_developments.description: Iraq War 19 March - 1 May 2003 — collapse of Saddam Hussein regime + transition to post-Saddam Iraqi federal framework.

key_developments.title: Constitution of Iraq + Article 117 federal recognition
key_developments.description: Constitution of Iraq adopted 15 October 2005 + Article 117 formally recognising Kurdistan Region as constitutionally-recognised federal region.

key_developments.title: Kurdistan Family-Law Amendment 15/2008
key_developments.description: Kurdistan-specific Family-Law Amendment 15/2008 modifying Iraqi Personal Status Law 188/1959 for Kurdistan Region.

key_developments.title: Kurdistan Independence Referendum
key_developments.description: Kurdistan Independence Referendum 25 September 2017 — 92.73% YES with 72.16% turnout.

key_developments.title: Iraqi Federal Supreme Court 2022 Ruling on 2017 Referendum
key_developments.description: Iraqi Federal Supreme Court ruling 22 February 2022 declaring 2017 Kurdistan Independence Referendum unconstitutional.

structural_findings: Kurdistan Region of Iraq operates a mixed federal Iraqi civil-law/Sharia-law + Kurdistan-specific semi-autonomous region framework — places Kurdistan in the Northern Iraqi semi-autonomous-region cluster.

Only sub-state-level Kurdish jurisdiction with formal constitutional autonomy framework is structurally distinctive globally — Iraqi Constitution 2005 Article 117 formally recognising Kurdistan Region.

Only sub-state-level Kurdish jurisdiction with continuous de facto independence framework predating constitutional recognition is structurally distinctive globally — 14 years of de facto autonomy 1991-2005 prior to formal constitutional recognition.

Central jurisdiction of contested 2017 Kurdistan independence referendum framework is structurally distinctive globally — 92.73% YES with 72.16% turnout, characterised by Iraqi Federal Government + Iran + Turkey as unilateral non-binding action.

Central jurisdiction of one of most distinctive modern post-Saddam constitutional federal frameworks is structurally distinctive globally — post-2003 Iraq War + 2005 Iraqi Constitution federal framework.

Anfal genocide 1986-1989 + Halabja chemical attack 1988 historical framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern sub-state-level Kurdish jurisdiction with formal genocide-recognition framework.
1991 Gulf War No-Fly Zone framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern sub-state-level Kurdish jurisdiction with formal UNSC-resolution-based de facto autonomy framework.
editorial_notes: Kurdistan Region of Iraq jurisdiction sidecar — mixed federal Iraqi civil-law/Sharia-law + Kurdistan-specific Northern Iraqi semi-autonomous region (Iraqi Constitution 2005 Article 117 + Iraqi Personal Status Law 188/1959 + Kurdistan Family-Law Amendment 15/2008 + Kurdistan Independence Referendum 2017 + Iraqi Federal Supreme Court 2022 ruling + UNSC Resolution 688/1991 + No-Fly Zone framework + Anfal genocide 1986-1989 + Halabja chemical attack 1988 + KRG established 1992 + 2003 Iraq War + Iraqi non-Hague-1980). Only sub-state-level Kurdish jurisdiction with formal constitutional autonomy framework globally + only sub-state-level Kurdish jurisdiction with continuous de facto independence framework predating constitutional recognition + central jurisdiction of contested 2017 Kurdistan independence referendum framework + central jurisdiction of one of most distinctive modern post-Saddam constitutional federal frameworks + Anfal genocide + Halabja chemical attack historical framework.
PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator.

Joins Northern-Iraqi + mixed Iraqi-civil-law/Sharia-law + Iraqi-semi-autonomous-region cluster + Iraqi-Constitution-2005-Article-117-globally-distinctive + Kurdistan-Independence-Referendum-2017-92-73-percent-YES + de-facto-autonomy-1991-2005-No-Fly-Zone + KRG-established-1992 + Anfal-genocide-1986-1989 + Halabja-chemical-attack-1988 + post-2003-Iraq-War-federal-framework + Iraqi-Federal-Supreme-Court-2022-ruling + Iraqi-non-Hague-1980 clusters within the corpus.
